summary: Matched a broad set of `mn` menu record/statistics functions across VS Records, Name Entry, Vibration, Sound Test, Data Delete, Info, Snap, and Count menus. The PR produced 21 new matches according to the decomp.dev report, including full matches for several functions and data sections, and improved adjacent unmatched logic in `mncount`, `mninfo`, and `mnruleplus`.
what_changed: Matched `mn_80232D4C`, `AddCharacterToName`, `mnNameNew_8023E0D8`, `mnDiagram2_GetStatValue`, `mnDiagram2_UpdateScrollArrows`, four `mnVibration` functions, `mnSoundTest_8024A790`, `mnDataDel_80250170`, `mnInfo_80251D58`, `mnInfo_80251F04`, `mnInfo_802522B8`, `mnSnap_80253BE0`, `fn_80257D7C`, and `mnCount_GetRowValue_Character` per the PR summary.

Factored repeated unlocked-character and unlocked-stage counting logic in `mncount.c` into `static inline` helpers `mnCount_CountUnlockedChars` and `mnCount_CountUnlockedMaps`, also improving `mnCount_GetRowValue_Number`.
Adjusted `mndiagram` prototypes and call sites so `is_name_mode` is passed as `int` from external callers while preserving explicit `(u8)` tests where needed for matching.

Introduced overlay data-layout structs such as `MnDiagram2DataLayout`, `MnInfoDataLayout`, `MnNameNewDataLayout`, and `MnVibrationDataLayout` to treat packed static data as structured fields for animations, strings, SIS IDs, and format strings.

Reworked `mnDataDel_80250170` to use local `HSD_Archive* archive` and `StaticModelDesc* assets`, indexing `assets[0..2]` when loading archive sections instead of spelling each global descriptor directly.

Moved or exposed several static data items and exact literals, including animation settings, string constants, `SDATA` assert strings for `jobj.h`/`jobj`, and float suffix fixes such as `-0.1F` in `mndatadel.static.h`.

Updated `mnVibration` to add helpers for name-slot and rumble lookup, local JObj translation helpers, exact `HSD_ASSERT` behavior for included `jobj.h`, and corrected signatures/data initializers needed to match `.data` and `.sdata`.
Adjusted `mnSnap` cleanup and cursor movement logic, including using `snap->cursor_gobj` and block-scoped loops for freeing text arrays, matching `mnSnap_80253BE0` and `fn_80257D7C`.
smart_moves: Used small `static inline` helpers to remove duplicated loops while still preserving matching codegen in nearby switch cases.

Modeled anonymous packed static blobs as layout structs, letting code reference `layout->date_format`, `layout->time_format`, or `base->anim[1]` instead of hard-coded adjacent globals; this is a reusable tactic when data symbols are really slices of one contiguous layout.
Inserted typed temporary variables such as `u16 val16` in `mnDiagram2_GetStatValue` so multiple switch cases share the same load/return shape instead of returning struct fields directly.
Used explicit casts like `(u8) is_name_mode`, `(s32) (u8) name_idx`, and `(u16) sp16` to force the original narrowing behavior.
Handled a header/prototype mismatch with `#define MNDIAGRAM_SOURCE` and conditional declarations in `mndiagram.h`, avoiding a one-size-fits-all prototype that would perturb source or caller codegen.
Kept archive-loading call shape close to the original by introducing locals (`archive`, `assets`) and array indexing, which reduced expression complexity and matched register allocation.
Matched library/assert side effects by temporarily redefining `HSD_ASSERT` around `baselib/jobj.h` include in `mnvibration.c`, preserving expected assert-file/assert-expression string data.
Tracked stack padding deliberately, removing, reducing, or adding `PAD_STACK` only where the diff showed the function needed a different frame shape.
decomp_lessons: For menu record/stat functions, direct field returns may fail to match; loading into an intermediate of the original width (`u16 val16`) can reproduce the compiler's register and extension sequence.

When a static symbol appears to contain unrelated animation settings, strings, and IDs, consider declaring a data-layout struct over the first symbol rather than treating each address as a separate independent object.
Conditional prototypes can be a practical decomp workaround when the defining translation unit and external callers require different apparent parameter types for matching.
Archive section loading often matches better when asset descriptors are treated as an array of `StaticModelDesc` and addressed with indexed fields.
Exact float literal spelling and suffixes can affect data matching; this PR changed literals like `-0.1` to `-0.1F` and similar Vec3 initializers.
The decomp.dev report is useful for catching regressions during broad matching PRs; the author explicitly paused to clean regressions before marking ready.
For functions manipulating `HSD_GObj` user data, using helper accessors like `HSD_GObjGetUserData(gobj)` may match better than direct `gobj->user_data` in some contexts.
naming_conventions: New local helpers use subsystem-prefixed CamelCase names: `mnCount_CountUnlockedChars`, `mnCount_CountUnlockedMaps`, `mnInfo_CountUnlocked`, `mnVibration_GetNameSlot`, `mnVibration_GetNameRumble`.
Layout structs are named after the owning module plus `DataLayout`, e.g. `MnDiagram2DataLayout`, `MnInfoDataLayout`, `MnNameNewDataLayout`, `MnVibrationDataLayout`.
Short generic helpers in `mnruleplus.c` use descriptive action names, e.g. `SisLib_ClearText` and `mnRulePlus_GetDescIdx`.
Existing address-based symbol names were preserved where no semantic name was established, such as `mnVibration_80248ED4`, `fn_80257D7C`, and `mn_80232D4C`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `static inline` helper functions to share logic without introducing out-of-line calls.
Use explicit integer-width casts in conditions and return paths to reproduce original truncation/extension.
Introduce locals only to influence register allocation and call argument ordering, as in `mnDataDel_80250170` and `mnSoundTest_8024A790`.
Use typed overlay structs over contiguous static data for animation arrays, SIS IDs, format strings, and other packed resources.

Adjust `PAD_STACK` by function based on frame-size evidence; examples include removing `PAD_STACK(8)` in `mnCount_GetRowValue_Number`, changing stack padding in `mnInfo`, and keeping `PAD_STACK(24)` where needed.
Use exact string/data placement for asserts and archive section names, including `SDATA` strings in `mnvibration.c`.
Wrap `baselib/jobj.h` inclusion with a local `HSD_ASSERT` definition to match the target assert metadata.
Use block scopes around loops and temporaries when necessary to alter lifetime/register reuse, as seen in `mnsnap.c` cleanup logic.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported `+7344` matched code bytes and `+624` matched data bytes, with 21 new matches and 6 improvements in unmatched items.
The author commented: "cleaning up regressions now. will mark ready once report is clean", indicating iteration against the match report before merge.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove address-based names from matched `mn` menu record functions.
Search for other menu modules using contiguous static data that could benefit from `DataLayout` overlay structs.
Audit remaining `mninfo` and `mnruleplus` unmatched improvements mentioned by the bot report: `fn_80252548`, `fn_80251FE4`, `mn_80233218`, and `.data`/`.sdata2` sections.
Look for other conditional header prototype patterns similar to `MNDIAGRAM_SOURCE`.
Search for other `baselib/jobj.h` include sites that require local `HSD_ASSERT` redefinition for matching.
